name: Weak equivalence principle (universality of free fall)
requirement: Any candidate framework must respect the weak equivalence principle - inertial mass equals gravitational mass for all matter - to the precision set by the MICROSCOPE satellite mission. Two test masses of different composition (titanium and platinum) must fall identically in Earth's gravitational field to better than ~1.5 x 10^-15 in the Eotvos parameter eta = 2 |a_1 - a_2| / |a_1 + a_2|.

regime.description: Earth-orbit-scale gravitational field, sustained free-fall inside a satellite.

current_bound.reference: Touboul et al., 'MICROSCOPE Mission: Final Results of the Test of the Equivalence Principle', PRL 129, 121102 (2022), arXiv:2209.15487

what_it_excludes: Any framework predicting composition-dependent fifth forces or equivalence-principle-violating couplings of dark sectors to ordinary matter at levels above eta ~ 10^-15. This severely constrains models that try to explain dark matter or dark energy through new long-range forces with non-universal couplings (e.g. baryon-coupled scalars).

parameterization.name: Eotvos parameter eta = 2 (a_1 - a_2) / (a_1 + a_2) for two test masses of different composition. In dilaton/EP-violating models, parameterized by a coupling Q_hat between a new scalar and ordinary matter.
parameterization.reference: Damour and Donoghue, PRD 82, 084033 (2010), arXiv:1007.2792 (dilaton parameterization).
parameterization.summary: WEP violations in any framework with new scalar/vector fields show up as composition-dependent accelerations. The dilaton parameterization separates 'how strongly the new field couples' from 'how composition-dependent the coupling is'.

procedural.predicted_observable: eta_AB = (Q_A - Q_B) * Q_hat for materials A, B, where Q_A, Q_B are dilaton charges per unit mass computed from nuclear and atomic structure. For MICROSCOPE: A = titanium (Ti), B = platinum (Pt), with Q_Ti - Q_Pt approximately 8 x 10^-4 in standard dilaton charge units.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: |eta_TiPt| < 1.5 x 10^-15 (1 sigma central value), |eta| < 4.7 x 10^-15 at 95% CL. Translates into bounds on dilaton coupling |Q_hat| <~ 10^-12 for typical dilaton models.
framework_inputs: If the framework introduces a new long-range scalar/vector field, compute its couplings to electromagnetism, QCD, and Yukawa sectors at the matching scale.
Convert these into dilaton charges per unit mass for Ti and Pt using the standard composition formulas (Damour-Donoghue).
Multiply by overall coupling Q_hat to predict eta.
procedural.notes: Frameworks with no long-range non-gravitational fields trivially predict eta = 0. Strong equivalence principle violations (Nordtvedt effect) are constrained separately by lunar laser ranging and pulsar timing.

primary_references.citation: Touboul et al. (MICROSCOPE), 'MICROSCOPE Mission: Final Results of the Test of the Equivalence Principle', PRL 129, 121102 (2022)
Will, 'The Confrontation between General Relativity and Experiment', Living Rev. Relativ. 17, 4 (2014)
